name: Depleted Uranium Oxide Removal Dataset
description: Comprehensive contamination dataset for Depleted Uranium Oxide. Includes chemical composition, safety metrics, PPE requirements, and laser removal parameters for effective contamination removal.

measurementTechnique: Laser contamination removal analysis, safety assessment, chemical characterization

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Depleted Uranium Oxide
contaminant.description: Uranium oxide forms on surfaces after exposure to nuclear materials in manufacturing and military settings. Unlike rust or dust, it exhibits dense layering and radioactivity so distinguishes itself clearly. On steel, bond holds tightly, while on concrete, penetration deepens into structure, and challenges arise from hardness during removal. Laser cleaning succeeds because ablation vaporizes contamination precisely without residue spread.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Uranium Oxide (as U), Uranium Dioxide (UO₂), Uranium Trioxide (UO₃) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
